negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The audited v2.2 port lacks audio_escrow_lockup/video_escrow_lockup and does not require worst-case audio/video executor escrow plus commitment, inclusion, and callback costs during wallet validation.
related_but_insufficient_code: wallet.rs supports HTTP, long-running HTTP, ZK, LLM, DKMS, persistent agent, and sovereign agent, but not AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE or VIDEO_CALL_PRECOMPILE.
ritual-precompile-addresses registers audio/video metadata, but both are short_settlement_enabled=false and payload/block verification reject them as unsupported two-phase fee families.
ritual-revm-internal precompile docs describe audio/video outputs, but do not implement v2.2 port wallet escrow checks.

notes: The short async wallet helper currently excludes audio and video even though those precompile addresses are registered.
rationale: Wallet validation now imports audio/video escrow helpers and uses audio_escrow_lockup or video_escrow_lockup for the executor fee branch, and includes audio/video in the long-running Phase 2 callback-cost path. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Wallet validation for audio and video async calls must require worst-case executor escrow plus the normal commitment, inclusion, and Phase 2 callback costs before accepting the original async transaction.
suggested_falsification_tests: For audio and video async calls, run wallet validation with balances just below and just above the sum of modality escrow, commitment fee, inclusion fee, and decoded delivery callback cost; assert only the sufficient balance case is accepted.
summary: Audio/video wallet escrow is not implemented in the audited v2.2 port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For audio and video async calls, run wallet validation with balances just below and just above the sum of modality escrow, commitment fee, inclusion fee, and decoded delivery callback cost; assert only the sufficient balance case is accepted.
title: Wallet escrow covers audio and video
